2605.20072.title: Probing Embodied LLMs: When Higher Observation Fidelity Hurts Problem Solving
2605.20072.paper_summary: Large Language Models are increasingly proposed as cognitive components for robotic systems, yet their opaque decision processes make it difficult to explain success or failure in closed-loop embodied tasks. Following an empirical AI methodology, we study embodied LLM agents behaviorally by varying the information available to the agent and measuring the resulting changes in behavior. Using the Lockbox, a sequential mechanical puzzle with hidden interdependencies, we evaluate LLMs across RGB, RGB-D, and ground-truth symbolic observations in a physical robotic setup and use controlled simulation to probe the resulting behavior. Counterintuitively, agents perform best under raw RGB input and worst under perfect ground-truth observations. In simulation, we probe this effect by randomly flipping perceived action outcomes and find that moderate noise improves performance, peaking at a 40% flip probability with a 2.85-fold success rate increase over the noise-free baseline. Further analysis links this gain to a reduction in repetitive action loops. These findings suggest that success rates alone are insufficient for evaluating LLMs, as measured performance may reflect the interaction between perceptual errors and reasoning failures rather than robust problem solving.
2605.20072.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被提議作為機器人系統的認知組件，然而它們不透明的決策過程使得在封閉回路具身任務中解釋成功或失敗變得困難。根據實證人工智慧方法論，我們通過改變可用於代理的資訊並測量行為的變化來行為上研究具身的LLM代理。使用鎖箱，一種具有隱藏相互依賴性的序列機械謎題，我們在物理機器人設置中評估LLM在RGB、RGB-D和真實符號觀察中的表現，並使用受控模擬來探查結果行為。出乎意料的是，代理在原始RGB輸入下表現最佳，而在完美的真實觀察下表現最差。在模擬中，我們通過隨機翻轉感知的行動結果來探查這一效應，發現適度的噪音能改善性能，在40%的翻轉概率下，成功率較無噪音基線提高了2.85倍。進一步分析將這一增益與重複行動循環的減少聯繫起來。這些發現表明，僅僅依賴成功率來評估LLM是不夠的，因為測得的性能可能反映了感知錯誤與推理失敗之間的互動，而不是穩健的問題解決能力。
2605.20072.author: Oussama Zenkri et.al.
